summary: Descriptive overview of homicide in substantive law: murder degrees, manslaughter, felony murder rule.
key_points: murder degrees is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for homicide are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine homicide in litigation.
The doctrine of homicide has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding homicide requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
